response.justification: Bogdan is a technically capable individual contributor with solid distributed-systems and backend experience, but the gap between his profile and this CTO role is fundamental and structural, not incremental. The role requires demonstrated executive leadership — scaling engineering orgs from 10 to 30+ people, developing senior technical leadership layers, communicating roadmaps to boards, and governing outsourced engineering — none of which appear anywhere in his history. His AI-agent exposure (MCP tooling at re:cinq, April 2025–present) is too recent and too shallow to satisfy the hard requirement for production multi-agent AI systems at commercial scale. Fintech experience is limited to a single IC role at a relatively modest investment platform. His career is entirely an individual-contributor trajectory at the Staff/Senior level; there is no evidence he has ever managed, hired, or been accountable for an engineering organization.
strengths: Genuine distributed-systems depth: production microservices at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second, with low-latency async I/O experience
Hands-on exposure to agentic-AI infrastructure and MCP tooling in his current role, giving at least surface familiarity with the architectural problem space
Broad polyglot background (TypeScript, D, C/C++, Node.js, Kubernetes, event-driven architecture) demonstrating adaptability across stacks
concerns: Zero engineering leadership track record: no people management, no team scaling, no org-building — every role is IC or solo founder of an open-source hobby project
No executive or VP-level experience; communicating technical strategy to a board, setting engineering governance, or owning P&L accountability are entirely absent from the resume

AI and fintech depth are both shallow and recent: the only AI-agent work started one month before the resume date, and fintech exposure is a single mid-level IC role with no credit-risk, payment-flow, or regulatory-compliance scope
